entry.title: [RBX/SBG][Containers & Orchestration] - Managed Rancher Service maintenance notification
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Jun 8, 07:30 - 16:00 UTC

Apr 22, 17:38 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, maintenance is scheduled on our Containers & Orchestration offer.

This may temporarily affect availability.

Start time : 08/06/2026 07:30 UTC
End time : 08/06/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : None
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, we need to upgrade Ranchers in version 2.11 to version 2.12. You can already manage the upgrade by yourself through OVHcloud Control panel or API.
The Rancher compatibility matrix with downstream clusters will change, indeed K8S version 1.30 will no longer be officially supported.
For more information regarding Managed Rancher Service supported versions and lifecycle policy, you can visit this page: KB0064608

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [Global][Data & Analytics ] - Data Platform maintenance notification
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Jun 1, 12:00 - 15:00 UTC

May 20, 20:18 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, a maintenance is scheduled on our Data Platform offer.

Here are the details of the maintenance:
Start time : 01/06/2026 12:00 UTC
End time : 01/06/2026 15:00 UTC
Service impact : None
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, we will be doing a maintenance on our Data Platform offer.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [GLOBAL][Containers & Orchestration] - End of life Kubernetes 1.30 notification
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT May 26, 07:00 UTC  -  May 28, 16:00 UTC

Apr 15, 14:18 UTC
Update - We are sorry to inform you that this maintenance is postponed.

As part of our continuous improvement plan, maintenance is scheduled on our Managed Kubernetes Service.

Start time : 26/05/2026 07:00 UTC
End time : 28/05/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : Customers have to anticipate this maintenance in order to adapt to the new Kubernetes version.
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, customers using Kubernetes version 1.30 will be migrated to version 1.31.

Thank you for your understanding.

Mar 18, 21:56 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, maintenance is scheduled on our Managed Kubernetes Service.

Start time : 26/05/2026 07:00 UTC
End time : 28/05/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : Customers have to anticipate this maintenance in order to adapt to the new Kubernetes version.
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, customers using Kubernetes version 1.30 will be migrated to version 1.31.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [GRA9/GRA11][Public Cloud] - Compute Instance (A10 Flavor) Maintenance Notification
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT May 25, 07:00 - 11:00 UTC

May 11, 15:36 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, we will be carrying out a maintenance on our electrical infrastructure in GRA9/GRA11 regions.

Start time : 25/05/2026 07:00 UTC
End time : 25/05/2026 11:00 UTC
Service impact : Instances will be unavailable during the maintenance. The only flavor impacted is A10.
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, we will be doing a maintenance on our electrical infrastructure.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [GLOBAL][AI & Machine Learning] - AI Endpoint incident notification
entry.content:
May 20, 15:39 UTC
Resolved - We are pleased to inform you that the incident affecting our AI & Machine Learning offering for gpt-oss-120b model has been resolved.

Start time : 19/05/2026 09:00 UTC
End time : 20/05/2026 12:20 UTC
Root Cause : This incident was caused by a software issue.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 20, 09:34 UTC
Investigating - We are currently investigating an incident affecting our AI & Machine Learning offering, which is causing temporary availability issue on AI Endpoint .

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 19/05/2026 09:00 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : The gpt-oss-120b model on AI Endpoints may have some performance issue.
Customers Impact : Customers may have temporarily difficulties to use the gpt-oss-120b model on AI Endpoints.
Ongoing Actions : Our teams are investigating to determine the origin of the incident and fix it.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

entry.title: [SBG5][Compute - Instance] - Public Cloud Incident Notification
entry.content:
May 20, 10:36 UTC
Resolved - We are pleased to inform you that the incident affecting our Compute - Instance offering has been resolved.

Start time : 20/05/2026 08:54 UTC
End time : 20/05/2026 10:16 UTC
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 20, 09:49 UTC
Update - Please find below an update on the situation:

Update :
Some Instances are once again reachable.
Our team remains committed to restoring the missing instances as quickly as possible.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 20, 09:21 UTC
Identified - We are currently experiencing an ongoing incident. We have determined the origin of the issue affecting our Compute - Instance offer in SBG5 region.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 20/05/2026 08:54 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Some instances in the SBG5 region are unreachable.
Customers Impact : Some customers are temporarily unable to access instances located in the specified region.
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected infrastructure failure
Ongoing Actions : The incident has been identified and our teams are mobilised to restore service as quickly as possible.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

Start time : 19/05/2026 04:46 UTC
End time : 19/05/2026 05:55 UTC
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 19, 05:01 UTC
Investigating - We are currently investigating an incident affecting our Storage offering, which is causing temporary availability issue in the region GRA5.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 19/05/2026 04:46 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : The block storage service is temporarily unavailable for some customers in GRA5 region.
Customers Impact : Some customers are temporarily unable to access and use their block Storage located in GRA5 region.
Ongoing Actions : Our teams are investigating to determine the origin of the incident and fix it.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

entry.title: [EU-SOUTH-MILA/B/C][Compute - Instance] - Instance creation incident notification
entry.content:
May 18, 09:58 UTC
Resolved - We are pleased to inform you that the incident affecting our Compute - Instance offering in regions EU-SOUTH-MIL-A, EU-SOUTH-MIL-B and EU-SOUTH-MIL-C has been resolved.

Start time : 15/05/2026 12:10 UTC
End time : 18/05/2026 09:54 UTC
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 18, 09:29 UTC
Identified - We are currently experiencing an ongoing incident. We have determined the origin of the issue affecting our Compute - Instance offer on the specific regions EU-SOUTH-MIL-A, EU-SOUTH-MIL-B and EU-SOUTH-MIL-C.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 15/05/2026 12:10 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Creation of Public Cloud on vRack and LoadBalancer is temporarily unavailable in EU-SOUTH-MIL-C region.
Since 18/05/2026 09:15 UTC, EU-SOUTH-MIL-A and EU-SOUTH-MIL-B regions are no longer impacted and creation of instances in these regions is available again.
Customers Impact : Customers are temporarily unable to create new Public Cloud instances vRack and LoadBalancer in EU-SOUTH-MIL-C region.
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.
Ongoing Actions : The incident has been identified and our teams are mobilised to restore service as quickly as possible.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 18, 09:17 UTC
Investigating - We are currently experiencing an event affecting our Compute - Instance offer in regions EU-SOUTH-MIL-A, EU-SOUTH-MIL-B and EU-SOUTH-MIL-C.

Our teams are fully committed to investigating this issue and working towards a resolution as soon as possible. As investigations are ongoing, we will share any new findings or updates with you as soon as possible.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

entry.title: [EU-WEST-PAR][Containers & Orchestration] - Managed Kubernetes Service update
entry.content:

May 11, 09:00 UTC
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 11, 08:48 UTC
Update - We will be undergoing scheduled maintenance during this time.

May 11, 08:41 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, maintenance is scheduled on our Managed Kubernetes Service offer.

Start time : 11/05/2026 09:00 UTC
End time : 11/05/2026 15:30 UTC
Service impact : None
Service improvement : Following our continuous improvement policy, we are rolling out an update to all cluster in a supported versions (1.34, 1.35) with an update policy of ALWAYS_UPDATE to the latest patch version.

Thank you for your understanding.

May 2, 16:24 UTC
Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.

May 2, 12:25 UTC

Start time : 02/05/2026 12:18 UTC

Customers Impact : Customers are temporarily unable to access instances located on the specified regions and Redis Database.
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected infrastructure failure
Ongoing Actions : The incident has been identified and our teams are mobilised to restore service as quickly as possible.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.
